title: Is combined NLL a sum of individual NLL?

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I do combined fit of three independent regions using MutiDimFit.
Can I say that combined NLL = NLL1+NLL2+NLL3 ?
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I do combined fit of three independent regions using MutiDimFit.
Can I say that combined NLL = NLL1+NLL2+NLL3 ?

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Hi Sasha,
I think the statistical committee would be better placed to answer, but I believe that’s correct, yes. That’s basically like taking the weighted average of independent measurements.
Although even if the regions are mutually exclusive, I would expect some parameters like luminosity or detector effects to be correlated, simply because all data are taken with the same experiment, so I don’t think this actually applies to CMS measurements except for very fringe cases.
Cheers,
Giacomo

Hi Sasha,

I think the statistical committee would be better placed to answer, but I believe that’s correct, yes. That’s basically like taking the weighted average of independent measurements.
Although even if the regions are mutually exclusive, I would expect some parameters like luminosity or detector effects to be correlated, simply because all data are taken with the same experiment, so I don’t think this actually applies to CMS measurements except for very fringe cases.

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Hi Giacomo,
thank you for answer !
My regions are mutually exclusive and fit is based purely on data using
signal and background modes. Bkg. models found from data sidebands.
So, I think technically in combine  total NLL is a sum of NLLs for each individual regions.
Cheers, Sasha.
